warnings: ops lifecycle is simulation evidence, not G5 product-ops closure evidence
resilience replay is simulation evidence, not real T4 node-loss evidence
stage replication is simulation evidence, not real added-node scaling evidence

decision: Use two local H100 GPUs as logical hosts for the current Phase 4 resilience/elasticity proxy gate; defer formal heterogeneous T4 validation.

deferred_requirements.reason: current evidence replays deterministic in-flight requests in simulation; real node power/network loss remains T4 lab work
current added-node evidence is simulated stage replication over two local logical hosts
AMD GPU node is not present on this development machine
Apple Silicon Mac is not present on this development machine
operator lifecycle hooks are simulated and auditable but not wired to a production deployment

evidence_checks.evidence: single-node-loss replay completes every in-flight request without dropped or duplicate tokens
evidence_checks.name: zero-dropped-in-flight

evidence_checks.evidence: stage replication uses added capacity, improves modeled makespan, and preserves output parity
evidence_checks.name: added-node-scaling

evidence_checks.evidence: operator deploy, drain, restart, rollback, and node replacement hooks are auditable
evidence_checks.name: drain-restart-rollback-hooks

evidence_checks.evidence: runbook defines node-loss, added-node, lifecycle, and heterogeneous-lab follow-up evidence collection
evidence_checks.name: t4-runbook-defined

evidence_checks.evidence: current evidence is simulation/proxy scope suitable for development with two local H100 logical hosts
evidence_checks.name: two-h100-proxy-scope

evidence_checks.evidence: formal_g4_passed remains false until real T4 node-loss and added-node evidence is attached
evidence_checks.name: no-formal-g4-overclaim

runbook.formal_gate_rule: G4 can pass only with real lab node-loss and added-node evidence; two-H100 local proxy evidence may pass only the current development proxy gate.
runbook.proxy_development_scope: two local H100 GPUs may stand in for two logical hosts until AMD/Mac lab hardware is available
required_artifacts: stage replication or added-node scaling artifact

scenarios.goal: prove in-flight requests survive loss of one serving/stage node
pass_criteria: completed_tokens match reference_tokens for every replayed request
setup: start from a validated placement over at least two logical hosts
record request ids, plan id, KV/checkpoint owner, and active node ownership before fault injection
disable new admission to the failed node before replay scheduling if the control plane is reachable

scenarios.goal: prove adding a replica increases modeled bottleneck-stage throughput without correctness regression
setup: capture baseline bottleneck-stage makespan with one replica
admit a second logical host as a stage replica
assign microbatches across both replicas using deterministic scheduling

scenarios.goal: prove operator mutations are auditable and drain in-flight work before node changes
setup: load cluster, model, and placement operator configs
run deploy, upgrade, restart, rollback, and node replacement actions
record drain and health-check events around every mutation

scenarios.goal: turn proxy evidence into formal T4/G4 evidence once AMD and Apple systems are available
pass_criteria: formal_g4_passed remains false until real heterogeneous evidence is attached
zero dropped in-flight requests under real single-node loss
added-node throughput improves on real hardware
operator signs the G4 gate packet
setup: replace same-host logical H100 stand-ins with real NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple nodes
collect node-loss and added-node evidence with production transport and auth enabled
attach raw artifacts, hardware inventory, operator acceptance, and gate-review decision record

runbook.scope: G4 evidence collection for node loss, replay, added capacity, and operator lifecycle hooks
runbook.title: T4 Resilience and Elasticity Runbook
